name: ProseMirror
description: A toolkit for building rich-text editors on the web with a modular architecture

description.value: A toolkit for building rich-text editors on the web, bridging structured content editing with user-friendly WYSIWYG interfaces

github-stars.comment: Across the main repository; individual packages have separate star counts

framework.comment: Framework-agnostic; can be integrated with any framework

base-library.comment: ProseMirror is itself a foundational library

data-model.comment: Uses a custom JSON document model; can serialize to/from HTML

bundle-size.comment: ~75KB gzipped for core packages (view, state, model, transform); full example setup is ~207-227KB minified

headless.comment: prosemirror-model and prosemirror-transform can run server-side without DOM

realtime-collab.comment: Official prosemirror-collab module provides collaborative editing primitives

collab-protocol.comment: Uses Operational Transformation with central authority model

offline-support.comment: Collab module requires central authority; no built-in offline queue

change-tracking.comment: Not built-in; can be implemented via plugins tracking transactions

comments.comment: Not built-in; can be implemented as custom marks or decorations

tables.comment: Official prosemirror-tables module with rowspan/colspan, cell selection, and table commands

images.comment: Built into schema-basic; resize/positioning requires custom implementation

embeds.comment: Not built-in; can be implemented as custom node types

code-blocks.comment: Built into schema-basic; syntax highlighting requires integration (e.g., CodeMirror)

math-latex.comment: Not built-in; community packages like prosemirror-math available

mentions.comment: Not built-in; community packages like prosemirror-mentions available

emoji-picker.comment: Not built-in; can be implemented via prosemirror-suggest or input rules

slash-commands.comment: Not built-in; community package prosemirror-slash-menu available

drag-drop-blocks.comment: Not built-in; can be implemented via custom node views and drag handlers

nested-blocks.comment: Schema supports arbitrary nesting of block nodes

toolbar.comment: prosemirror-menu provides building blocks; no default toolbar UI

mobile-support.comment: Works on mobile browsers; some edge cases with virtual keyboards

keyboard-shortcuts.comment: prosemirror-keymap module for binding keyboard shortcuts

accessibility.comment: Uses contentEditable which provides native accessibility; ARIA can be added

find-replace.comment: Not built-in; can be implemented via search plugin

spell-check.comment: Browser native spell check works with contentEditable

plugin-system.comment: First-class plugin system with state management, view props, and transaction filtering

custom-nodes.comment: Schema-based; define custom block and inline node types

custom-marks.comment: Marks represent inline formatting; fully customizable

schema-customization.comment: Document schema is fully customizable and defines allowed content structure

themes.comment: No built-in theming; CSS styling is up to the implementer

documentation-quality.comment: Excellent documentation with comprehensive guide, API reference, and examples

npm-downloads.comment: prosemirror-view weekly downloads; core package for usage measurement

commercial-support.comment: No official commercial support; community forum available
